title: phi engine 110 -> training loss integration
rationale: Compute decoupled (engines offline, cache reused at ~0ms/step), no surrogate rewriting needed, clean gradient through projection head only, scales 1->110 uniformly
completed: Design doc: shared/papers/training_phi_integration_design.md (4 options analyzed, C recommended)
phi_cache_v1.jsonl: 10 state embeddings (113-dim each), Option A PoC data
clm_flag_on benchmark: phi_cache working, 13.75x speedup vs 27.5s baseline, loss 3.47->2.10 stable
clm_track_b_speedup: 630x speedup on isolated cache lookup (43ms vs 27.5s historical)
engine_integration.hexa (ALM-P23-1): 12-engine registry with weights/roles, self-test 5/5 PASS
not_yet_built: phi_engine_eval.hexa: wrapper to run engines and emit structured phi vectors (does not exist)
phi_harvest.hexa: offline anchor harvester (110 engines x N corpus samples -> phi_anchors cache)
PhiProjectionHead: h_last -> d_proj=64 projection for InfoNCE
phi_contrastive_loss: InfoNCE implementation in train loop
Full 110-engine anchor cache (current cache has only 10 entries, 113-dim, not 110-dim)
next_steps: 1. Build phi_engine_eval.hexa in anima/training/ — structured hexa wrapper that runs N engines and returns {engine_name: phi_value} JSON
2. Build phi_harvest.hexa — offline batch runner: iterate 110 engines over corpus chunks, write (sample_id -> phi_vector[110]) cache file
3. Extend engine_integration.hexa registry from 12 -> 110 engines (add domain grouping: cognitive/affective/clinical/altered-state/quantum/meta)
4. Wire PhiProjectionHead + InfoNCE loss into train_alm_14b.hexa generator (Python section)
5. Run r5 A/B test: loss = main_CE + 0.01*holo + 0.02*l_phi vs r4 baseline

anchor_harvest_infra.estimated_effort: 1 engineering day + ~1 CPU-hour per 10k corpus chunks
anchor_harvest_infra.blocker: phi_engine_eval.hexa must exist first to provide structured output from engines

current_cache_gap: phi_cache_v1.jsonl has 113-dim embeddings (state embeddings), not 110-dim engine phi vectors. Option C needs per-engine phi scalars harvested from all 110 engines, stored as (sample_id -> float[110])
risk: 110 engines have no smooth surrogate — Option C avoids this by treating phi outputs as read-only labels, gradient flows only through model projection head
